name: Southern Turnip Green Soup (Florida Cooking)
recipeIngredient: Salt and freshly ground pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the turnip roots off the greens. Clean and wash the greens, discarding any blemished or yellowed edges.
2. Tear the large leaves into smaller pieces.
3. Trim the ends and peel the turnips. Cube into bite-size pieces, enough to make 4 cups.
4. Melt 3 tablespoons of butter in a large skillet over moderate heat. Add the turnips and sauté until crisp-tender, about 10 minutes.
5. Add the onions, and sauté for 5 minutes or until tender, but not browned.
6. Add 1/2 teaspoon salt, 1/4 teaspoon sugar, and 1 1/2 cups of chicken stock. Blend well, stir in the lemon juice, and set aside.
7. In another skillet, melt the remaining 3 tablespoons of butter. Add the greens and sauté over medium heat for 10 minutes until crisp-tender.
8. Add 1 teaspoon salt, 1 teaspoon sugar, and the remaining 4 cups chicken stock. Blend well and remove from the heat, then let cool for 15 minutes.
9. Puree the greens in a food processor or in batches in a blender.
10. Add the pureed greens to the turnip mixture. Mix together and reheat.
11. Add the half-and-half and cooked grits, and adjust the salt and pepper to taste. Serve hot.
